DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL PIERCE'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FOR SANCTIONS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANT. DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE COMPLETE RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION AND REMIT $900 IN SANCTIONS BY MAY 13, 2011. CCP 2023.010(D), 2030.290(C), 2031.320(B). IN ADDITION, THE COURT STATED THAT IT DID NOT TAKE INTO ACCOUNT ANY EVIDENCE THAT WAS NOT BEFORE THE COURT. RE ISSUE OF SANCTIONS, COURT CLARIFIED IT IS AGAINST DEFENDANT AND ITS COUNSEL OF RECORD AS OF TODAY (05/06/11). JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; NOT REPORTED
SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY: BAIREY, JEFFERY GALT SUBSTITUTED FOR FITZGERALD, WILLIAM FRANCIS AS ATTORNEY FOR BLACK & VEATCH CORPORATION
ISSUING COMMISSION TO TAKE DEPOSITION OUT OF STATE UNDER CCP 2026 FILED BY DEFENDANT BLACK & VEATCH CORPORATION PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BLACK & VEATCH CORPORATION AND PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. DEFENDANTS SATISFIED THEIR BURDEN OF PRODUCTION AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED ANY EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE THAT EITHER DEFENDANT RETAINED CONTROL OF THE WORK PERFORMED BY PLAINTIFF OR AFFIRMATIVELY CONTRIBUTED TO PLAINTIFF'S INJURIES. Off Calendar Jan-17-2012 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS BLACK & VEATCH CORPORATION AND PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Appearances: Jeffery G. Bairey of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (438.6657) for defendant and moving parties; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. Defendants satisfied their burden of production and plaintiff has not presented any evidence creating a triable issue that either defendant retained control of the work performed by plaintiff or affirmatively contributed to plaintiff's injuries. Orders signed on summary judgment and on objections. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-22-2011 Continued To Feb-06-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Off Calendar Apr-22-2011 Continued To Jan-20-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 504

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-10-2011 Continued To Jun-24-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 504

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Response To Form Interrogatories And Req. For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF MICHAEL PIERCE'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FOR SANCTIONS.

APPEARANCES: Philip R. Weltin, Esq. of Weltin Streb & Weltin LLP 510/251-6060 for pltf & moving party; Alison Yew, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP 362-2580 & William F. Fitzgerald, Esq. of Narayan & Hudson 227-2328 for deft & opposing party Black & Veatch Corporation.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANT. DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE COMPLETE RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION AND REMIT $900 IN SANCTIONS BY MAY 13, 2011. CCP 2023.010(d), 2030.290(c), 2031.320(b). In addition, the Court stated that it did not take into account any evidence that was not before the Court. Re issue of sanctions, Court clarified it is against defendant and its counsel of record as of today (05/06/11). Judge: Marla J. Miller; Not reported

MISCELLANEOUS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Order to Show Cause to Defendant's Counsel William F. Fitzgerald for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures No. 4. (Administrative fee) is ordered off calendar and dissolved/Fee paid. Proceedings not reported. (610)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-03-2010 Set For Trial On Jul-11-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Jun-24-11 At 08:30 A.M. In Dept 504
